source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $AB=AC$, $\angle A=120^\circ$, $BC=12\,cm$. The perpendicular bisector of $AB$ intersects $BC$ at point M and intersects $AB$ at point E. The perpendicular bisector of $AC$ intersects $BC$ at point N and intersects $AC$ at point F. What is the length of $MN$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Draw $AM$ and $AN$, \\
since the perpendicular bisector of $AB$ intersects $BC$ at point $M$, and $AB$ at point $E$, and the perpendicular bisector of $AC$ intersects $BC$ at point $N$, and $AC$ at point $F$, \\
thus $AM=BM$, $AN=CN$, \\
therefore, $\angle BAM=\angle B$, $\angle CAN=\angle C$, \\
since $AB=AC$ and $\angle A=120^\circ$, \\
therefore, $\angle B=\angle C=30^\circ$, \\
thus, $\angle AMN=\angle ANM=60^\circ$, \\
therefore, $\triangle AMN$ is an equilateral triangle, \\
thus, $AM=MN=AN$, \\
thus, $BM=MN=BN$, \\
since $BC=12\text{cm}$, \\
therefore, $MN=\frac{1}{3}BC=\boxed{4\text{cm}}$.
